name: Attempt to bypass UAC via Windows Firewall Snap-In Hijacking
notes:
This rule detects attempts to bypass UAC via Windows firewall snap-in.

https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/security/current/uac-bypass-via-windows-firewall-snap-in-hijack.html
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
when the event matches LOWER("Parent Process Full Full Name") LIKE 'mmc.exe' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '%wf.msc%' AQL filter query
when the event matches LOWER("Process Full Full Name") == 'werfault.exe' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Attempt to bypass UAC via Windows Firewall Snap-In Hijacking on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
AND when the event matches LOWER("Parent Process Full Full Name") LIKE 'mmc.exe' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '%wf.msc%' AQL filter query
AND NOT when the event matches LOWER("Process Full Full Name") == 'werfault.exe' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Attempt to bypass UAC via Windows Firewall Snap-In Hijacking
Event description: Detects attempts to bypass UAC via Windows firewall snap-in.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
